gaps.requirement: Versioned per-provider selector manifest shipped as remote JSON data, with an out-of-band SLA fix path and locale/A-B-tolerant multi-candidate selectors (FR-BC6/BC7).
gaps.quote: Versioned selector manifest per provider, shipped as remote JSON data (FR-BC7) — a multi-candidate list preferring stable data-testid/ARIA-role/structural anchors over brittle hashed class names or localized ARIA-label/placeholder text ... a selector-data fix ships within the bounded FR-BC7/NFR-8 SLA, not store-review time.
gaps.why_uncovered: The E5 canary stories (ENG-2725 selector fixtures, ENG-2726 fail-loud/team-alert, covers FR-D7) own local breakage self-detection and alerting, but no story owns a remote data-delivery channel that ships a selector fix within the FR-BC7 SLA without a store update, nor the locale-variant tolerance.
gaps.verdict_reason: Source (5t8uxVnnA4 §B) canonical; not in feature-map.txt (no extension section), not in breakdown/extension/plan.json covers (E5-S2/ENG-2725 owns only local versioned fixtures, not a remote-fetched data channel), not in fix-specs.json ENG-2696..2710. Build work, not process. proposed_issue sane as written.

proposed_issue.title: [extension] Remote versioned selector-manifest channel — out-of-band SLA selector fix, locale/A-B tolerant (FR-BC6/BC7)
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 1 🎯 Story
As the extension delivery pipeline, I fetch a **versioned, per-provider selector manifest shipped as remote JSON data** (not baked into the bundle) so a selector-data fix ships within the FR-BC7/NFR-8 SLA — out-of-band, without a store-review round-trip — while MV3's no-remote-code rule (FR-TS4) stays intact.

**DoD test:** `selector-manifest.e2e` — with a stale bundled selector, publishing a new manifest version to the remote data endpoint makes the canary resolve the compose node on the next heartbeat with **zero extension re-install / store update**.
**⛔ Dispatch block:** Blocked until ENG-2690 freezes the manifest schema (provider → ordered candidate list) and the hosting/versioning contract.

## 2 ✅ Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Manifest is **data-only JSON** (selectors, candidate ordering, version, provider); no executable code is fetched — MV3 no-remote-code holds [Source: 5t8uxVnnA4 §B, FR-TS4].
- [ ] Each provider entry is a **multi-candidate list** preferring `data-testid` / ARIA-role / structural anchors over hashed class names AND over localized ARIA-label/placeholder text [Source: 5t8uxVnnA4 §B, FR-BC6].
- [ ] Manifest tolerates A/B **and** locale variants — an EU-beachhead fr/de DOM with different label strings does not false-trip the canary [Source: 5t8uxVnnA4 §B, FR-BC6].
- [ ] Manifest is **versioned**; the extension pins a floor version, fetches newer, and a bad manifest fails closed to the last-known-good (never bricks delivery).
- [ ] A selector-data fix reaches installed clients within the bounded FR-BC7/NFR-8 SLA; a **code-level** insertion-technique fix still routes through a store update [Source: 5t8uxVnnA4 §B, FR-BC7].

## 3 🔨 Tasks
- [ ] Define + host the versioned manifest endpoint (data-only), served to installed extensions.
- [ ] Client fetch + cache + version-pin + last-known-good fallback in the delivery/canary path.
- [ ] Wire the canary `resolve` step to consume manifest candidates in order.
- [ ] Locale-variant + A/B fixtures proving no false NOTFOUND across fr/de/en.

## 4 🧠 Context
- The E5 canary stories (ENG-2725 selector fixtures, ENG-2726 fail-loud/team-alert) own local breakage self-detection — they do **not** own a remote data-delivery channel that ships a selector fix without store review; that out-of-band SLA path (FR-BC7) is unowned.
- This is the mechanism that turns a provider-UI change from a store-review-time outage into a bounded-SLA data push.
- **🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 5 🧪 Test approach
- E2E: publish manifest v+1 → canary resolves on next heartbeat, no re-install.
- Negative: malformed manifest → fail-closed to last-known-good, delivery unaffected.
- Locale matrix fixtures (en/fr/de) assert no false NOTFOUND.

## 6 📏 Standards
- Coding Standards `6aMAzsYeQb` (`CS §`) — cite rules in the PR + review.
- SE-Architect Review Agent `8sYi523i4t` — adversarial lenses + Done gates.

## 7 🔗 References
- Addendum §B (breakage-canary design) `5t8uxVnnA4`
- PRD F1 `g9vWbSYplh` (FR-BC6/BC7, FR-TS4, NFR-8)

## 8 🔗 Dependencies
- Blocked by ENG-2690 (definition pack — manifest schema freeze).
- Feeds ENG-2725 / ENG-2726 (canary consumes the manifest).

## 9 📡 Protocol
On PR: cite `CS §` + the FR-BC6/BC7 sources; attach the locale-matrix run + the v+1 hot-fix E2E recording. Comment result on this issue and cross-ref ENG-2725.

gaps.requirement: Non-destructive insert — detect existing non-empty composer content and never overwrite an in-progress draft (FR-BC8).
gaps.quote: Non-destructive insert: before writing, detect existing non-empty composer content and never overwrite an in-progress draft (FR-BC8).
gaps.why_uncovered: E3 delivery adapters (ENG-2717/2718/2719, covers FR-D1) inject the prompt and E4 (ENG-2721, covers FR-D4) governs consent, but no story owns detecting a pre-existing draft and refusing to overwrite it; FR-BC8 is not in any story's covers set.
gaps.verdict_reason: Source (5t8uxVnnA4 §B, FR-BC8) canonical; grepped feature-map.txt (no hit, no extension section), breakdown/extension/plan.json + E3-S1/E4-S1/E4-S2 bodies (no draft-preservation check in any AC), fix-specs.json ENG-2696..2710 (no hit). Build work. Fixed: blocked_by was missing ENG-2690, which every sibling extension story (incl. ENG-2717 itself) cites as the dispatch gate.

proposed_issue.title: [extension] Non-destructive insert — never overwrite an in-progress composer draft (FR-BC8)
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 1 🎯 Story
As the delivery pipeline, before writing an enriched prompt I **detect existing non-empty composer content and never overwrite an in-progress draft** (FR-BC8), so a user who was mid-typing never loses their text to an injection.

**DoD test:** `nondestructive-insert.e2e` — with pre-existing text in the compose node, a delivery is refused/deferred (surfaced to the user), and the original draft is byte-for-byte intact.
**⛔ Dispatch block:** Blocked until ENG-2690 freezes the draft-detection contract and ENG-2717 lands the injection port whose write step this guards.

## 2 ✅ Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Before insert, the pipeline reads the compose node and classifies empty vs **non-empty draft** [Source: 5t8uxVnnA4 §B, FR-BC8].
- [ ] On a non-empty draft, the write is **not performed silently**; the user is offered append/replace/cancel — no overwrite without explicit choice.
- [ ] The guard runs on the resolved node **before** the write reaches the visible conversation (aligns with FR-BC3 abort-before-write) [Source: 5t8uxVnnA4 §B, FR-BC3].
- [ ] Guard holds across all GO providers (ChatGPT, Gemini) and both editor models (React textarea, ProseMirror/contenteditable) [Source: 5t8uxVnnA4 §A].

## 3 🔨 Tasks
- [ ] Add a pre-write draft-presence check to the shared injection port.
- [ ] Per-editor-model emptiness detection (textarea value vs PM/contenteditable node).
- [ ] Wire the append/replace/cancel decision into the consent surface.

## 4 🧠 Context
- The E3 adapters (ENG-2717/2718/2719) inject the enriched prompt; the E4 consent gate (ENG-2721) governs private-memory consent — neither owns draft-preservation. FR-BC8 non-destructive insert is a distinct delivery-safety obligation with no owner.
- Both fork/donor writers auto-inject silently; non-destructive insert is Orvex-specific build-fresh behaviour.
- **🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 5 🧪 Test approach
- E2E: mid-typed draft present → no silent overwrite; draft intact.
- E2E: empty composer → normal insert path.
- Cross-provider matrix (ChatGPT textarea + Gemini contenteditable).

## 6 📏 Standards
- Coding Standards `6aMAzsYeQb` (`CS §`) — cite rules in the PR + review.
- SE-Architect Review Agent `8sYi523i4t` — adversarial lenses + Done gates.

## 7 🔗 References
- Addendum §A/§B `5t8uxVnnA4` (FR-BC3, FR-BC8)
- PRD F1 `g9vWbSYplh`

## 8 🔗 Dependencies
- Blocked by ENG-2690 (draft-detection contract) + ENG-2717 (injection-port framework).
- Touches ENG-2721 (consent surface hosts the append/replace/cancel choice).

## 9 📡 Protocol
On PR: cite `CS §` + FR-BC8; attach the mid-draft E2E recording proving zero overwrite. Comment on this issue, cross-ref ENG-2717 + ENG-2721.

gaps.requirement: Orvex memory-enrichment backend wiring — the extension retrieves enrichment from Orvex knowledge/ai (replacing the forked supermemory / mem0 backends and their phone-home).
gaps.quote: Orvex memory-enrichment backend wiring (replace supermemory/mem0 backends with Orvex knowledge/ai). ... Strip: the api.mem0.ai phone-home (FR-TS2).
gaps.why_uncovered: No extension story covers the inbound retrieval call that fetches the enrichment payload to inject; the E3 adapters (ENG-2717..2720) inject an 'enriched prompt' but never define where it comes from, and ENG-2696 is the opposite-direction outbound vendor sync-out in the knowledge service.
gaps.verdict_reason: Source (5t8uxVnnA4 §E build plan) canonical; not in feature-map.txt, not in any breakdown/*/plan.json covers set (knowledge/ai plans have zero 'extension'/'enrich' hits — no server-side retrieval endpoint claims this either), not in fix-specs.json. Build work. Fixed: blocked_by cited ENG-2716 (E2-S3, connection-status surface) for 'the scoped token' — the scoped-token story is actually E2-S2 = ENG-2715 per ledger-extension.json; corrected throughout.

proposed_issue.title: [extension] Memory-enrichment retrieval — wire the delivery path to Orvex knowledge/ai, remove supermemory/mem0 backends (FR-TS2)
proposed_issue.milestone: NEW: B7 — Memory-enrichment retrieval & Orvex backend wiring
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 1 🎯 Story
As the extension, I **retrieve the memory enrichment from Orvex knowledge/ai** (replacing the forked supermemory / mem0 backends) using the scoped delegated token, so the prompt I inject is enriched from the user's Orvex Memory rather than any vendor phone-home.

**DoD test:** `enrichment-retrieval.e2e` — a connected user's compose context yields an enrichment payload fetched from Orvex knowledge/ai (no `api.mem0.ai` / supermemory host contacted, verified by network capture), which the delivery adapter then injects.
**⛔ Dispatch block:** Blocked until ENG-2715 provides the scoped token and ENG-2690 freezes the enrichment request/response contract.

## 2 ✅ Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Enrichment is fetched from **Orvex knowledge/ai**; the forked supermemory backend and the mem0 `api.mem0.ai` phone-home are removed (FR-TS2) [Source: 5t8uxVnnA4 §E].
- [ ] Retrieval uses the **scoped delegated token** from E2, never a raw user secret [Source: 5t8uxVnnA4 §A; g9vWbSYplh FR-D4].
- [ ] The retrieval boundary respects the personal↔employer firewall — it never pulls a memory the delivery-time firewall (ENG-2722) would forbid [Source: g9vWbSYplh FR-D4/FR-D5].
- [ ] Network capture in CI shows **only** Orvex hosts contacted from the enrichment path; a denylist gate fails the build on any vendor-backend host.

## 3 🔨 Tasks
- [ ] Replace the fork's backend client with an Orvex knowledge/ai enrichment client (contract from ENG-2690).
- [ ] Thread the scoped token; strip the mem0/supermemory phone-home code paths.
- [ ] Compose-context → enrichment request; hand the payload to the delivery adapter (ENG-2717).
- [ ] CI net-capture denylist for non-Orvex hosts on the enrichment path.

## 4 🧠 Context
- The extension epics cover auth (E2), delivery adapters (E3), consent/firewall (E4) and degradation (E5) — but none owns the **inbound** call that fetches the enrichment to inject. ENG-2696 is the **outbound** vendor sync-out (opposite direction), not this.
- This is an explicit build-fresh obligation: 'replace supermemory/mem0 backends with Orvex knowledge/ai'.
- **🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 5 🧪 Test approach
- E2E: connected user → enrichment fetched from Orvex, injected.
- Network assertion: no mem0/supermemory host contacted.
- Firewall: employer-scoped context never returns a personal-only memory.

## 6 📏 Standards
- Coding Standards `6aMAzsYeQb` (`CS §`) — cite rules in the PR + review.
- SE-Architect Review Agent `8sYi523i4t` — adversarial lenses + Done gates.

## 7 🔗 References
- Addendum §E (build plan / build-fresh) `5t8uxVnnA4`
- Build-vs-buy ruling `IgOjzk034v`
- PRD F1 `g9vWbSYplh` (FR-D4/D5, FR-TS2)

## 8 🔗 Dependencies
- Blocked by ENG-2715 (scoped token) + ENG-2690 (enrichment contract).
- Feeds ENG-2717 (adapter injects the retrieved payload); cross-ref ENG-2696 (sync-out, distinct).

## 9 📡 Protocol
On PR: cite `CS §` + §E; attach the network-capture proving Orvex-only hosts + the end-to-end enrich→inject recording. Comment on this issue, cross-ref ENG-2717 + ENG-2696.

gaps.requirement: SPA resilience — MutationObserver + URL-poll re-init to survive new-chat/model-switch navigation, plus data-orvex-initialized idempotency guards against double-injection.
gaps.quote: SPA resilience: MutationObserver + URL-poll re-init to survive new-chat / model-switch navigation without losing the composer reference; data-orvex-initialized idempotency guards to prevent double-injection across observer fires.
gaps.why_uncovered: E3-S1 (ENG-2717, covers FR-D1/FR-D5) defines the provider registry + injection port but no story names the SPA-navigation re-init lifecycle or the idempotency guard; without it delivery silently breaks on new-chat/model-switch.
gaps.verdict_reason: Source (5t8uxVnnA4 §A) canonical; grepped feature-map.txt (no hit), breakdown/extension E3-S1/E3-S2/E3-S3 bodies (no observer/re-init/idempotency AC), fix-specs.json (no hit). Build work. Fixed: blocked_by was missing ENG-2690, which the story's own DoD text ('exact ... pinned at ENG-2690') implies and every sibling story cites directly.

proposed_issue.title: [extension] SPA-navigation resilience — observer/URL re-init + idempotency guards against double-injection
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 1 🎯 Story
As a content script on a provider SPA, I **survive new-chat / model-switch navigation** via a `MutationObserver` + URL-poll re-init and guard against double-injection with `data-orvex-initialized` idempotency markers, so delivery keeps working after the composer is re-rendered and never fires twice.

**DoD test:** `spa-resilience.e2e` — after a new-chat navigation and a model switch (no full page load), the extension re-resolves the composer and delivers exactly once; observer re-fires do not produce a duplicate injection.
**⛔ Dispatch block:** Blocked until ENG-2690 freezes the re-init/idempotency contract and ENG-2717 lands the injection port whose lifecycle this manages.

## 2 ✅ Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A `MutationObserver` + URL-poll re-init re-acquires the composer reference across SPA navigation without a full reload [Source: 5t8uxVnnA4 §A].
- [ ] `data-orvex-initialized` idempotency guards prevent double-injection across repeated observer fires [Source: 5t8uxVnnA4 §A].
- [ ] A stale composer reference after navigation is detected and refreshed before the next delivery (never writes to a detached node).
- [ ] Re-init holds for both GO providers' SPA shells (ChatGPT, Gemini).

## 3 🔨 Tasks
- [ ] Add observer + URL-poll lifecycle to the injection framework.
- [ ] Idempotency marker on init; short-circuit re-entry.
- [ ] Stale-reference detection tied into the canary resolve step.

## 4 🧠 Context
- E3-S1 (ENG-2717) defines the injection port/registry but does not name the SPA-navigation lifecycle; without re-init the composer reference goes stale on new-chat/model-switch and delivery silently fails. No story owns this resilience behaviour.
- **🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 5 🧪 Test approach
- E2E: new-chat + model-switch → re-resolve + single delivery.
- Observer storm → no duplicate injection (idempotency).

## 6 📏 Standards
- Coding Standards `6aMAzsYeQb` (`CS §`) — cite rules in the PR + review.
- SE-Architect Review Agent `8sYi523i4t` — adversarial lenses + Done gates.

## 7 🔗 References
- Addendum §A (mechanism & transport) `5t8uxVnnA4`
- PRD F1 `g9vWbSYplh` (FR-D1)

## 8 🔗 Dependencies
- Blocked by ENG-2690 (re-init/idempotency contract) + ENG-2717 (injection-port framework).

## 9 📡 Protocol
On PR: cite `CS §` + §A; attach the navigation E2E recording proving re-resolve + single-fire. Comment on this issue, cross-ref ENG-2717.

gaps.requirement: Per-provider live-authenticated-DOM injection prototype as a pre-build gate, including the Gemini closed-shadow-DOM (FR-PA6) accessibility check that decides GO vs DEGRADE — the spike validated nothing against a live DOM.
gaps.quote: Live-DOM prototype REQUIRED before trust: the spike designed this against static/archived HTML; the Chrome browser tool was not connected, so no selector was validated against a live authenticated DOM. Prototype per provider — including, for Gemini, the closed-shadow-DOM check (FR-PA6, §C) — is a build precondition (§7).
gaps.why_uncovered: ENG-2689 (spike, In Review) only saw static/archived HTML with the Chrome tool disconnected; ENG-2690 owns the definition contract not the live-DOM execution; and E6-S2 (ENG-2728) is a post-build CI E2E regression harness, not the pre-build DOM-accessibility go/no-go gate (incl. FR-PA6 closed-shadow-DOM) that must decide Gemini GO vs DEGRADE before adapters are built.
gaps.verdict_reason: Underlying requirement is real (5t8uxVnnA4 §B canonical) and absent from feature-map.txt/breakdown/fix-specs.json per the specified search — but this exact proposed_issue duplicates gap-hunt/ext-sweep/verdict.json's '[extension] Live-DOM canary validation' ticket (same live-DOM-before-build test, same Gemini closed-shadow-DOM check, same GO providers), filed the same sweep/day. Filing both creates two tickets for one obligation. This copy is also the weaker of the two: blocked_by omits ENG-2690 (every sibling extension story cites it directly, and the addendum's own 2026-07-15 counsel note reaffirms ENG-2690/the contract TAG gates every build story) and its milestone (B3) self-contradicts the ticket's own stated role of blocking B3's adapters (ENG-2718/2719) before they're built. Recommend closing this candidate in favor of the verdict.json ticket rather than filing both.

proposed_issue.title: [extension] Live-DOM per-provider injection prototype gate — validate selectors + Gemini closed-shadow-DOM (FR-PA6) before adapter build
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 1 🎯 Story
As a build precondition, I run a **per-provider live-authenticated-DOM injection prototype** — validating each GO provider's selectors and composer-write technique against a real logged-in DOM, and explicitly ruling in/out **Gemini's closed-shadow-DOM (FR-PA6)** — before the full adapters are trusted, because the spike only saw static/archived HTML with the Chrome tool disconnected.

**DoD test:** `live-dom-proto` — for each GO provider a recorded live-DOM session shows the enriched text landing in the real composer via the native-setter/`InputEvent` path; the Gemini prototype records the shadow-root mode and returns an explicit GO or DEGRADE.
**⛔ Dispatch block:** Blocked until ENG-2734 counsel GO holds and ENG-2689 spike is closed; this gate must PASS before ENG-2718/2719 adapter build is trusted.

## 2 ✅ Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each GO provider's compose node is resolved + written against a **live authenticated DOM** (not static/archived HTML) [Source: 5t8uxVnnA4 §B].
- [ ] Gemini prototype records whether the Angular/Material composer renders inside a `mode:'closed'` shadow root; a closed root forces **DEGRADE regardless of ToS** (FR-PA6) [Source: 5t8uxVnnA4 §C].
- [ ] Read-back confirms the injected text registered with the editor model within the ~250ms budget on the live DOM (FR-BC1/BC2) [Source: 5t8uxVnnA4 §B].
- [ ] The prototype outputs the GO/DEGRADE decision per provider that feeds FR-PA3 and gates the adapter build [Source: 5t8uxVnnA4 §C].

## 3 🔨 Tasks
- [ ] Stand up a live authenticated-DOM harness (Chrome tool connected) per GO provider.
- [ ] Validate native-setter (React) + `execCommand insertText`/`beforeinput` (PM) + Gemini triple-fire against live DOM.
- [ ] Gemini shadow-root-mode probe (FR-PA6) → record + decide.
- [ ] Publish the per-provider GO/DEGRADE decision + validated selectors into the ENG-2690 contract.

## 4 🧠 Context
- ENG-2689 (viability spike, In Review) designed against static/archived HTML with the browser tool disconnected — no selector was validated live; the addendum makes the live-DOM prototype a hard build precondition (§7). ENG-2690 owns the definition contract, not the live-DOM execution; E6-S2 (ENG-2728) is the post-build CI E2E harness, not the pre-build accessibility go/no-go gate.
- FR-PA6 closed-shadow-DOM is a categorical, non-ToS block — it cannot be absorbed by the canary and must be ruled out before committing to a Gemini adapter.
- **🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 5 🧪 Test approach
- Live-DOM recording per provider showing successful enrich→inject.
- Gemini shadow-root-mode captured; DEGRADE path exercised if closed.

## 6 📏 Standards
- Coding Standards `6aMAzsYeQb` (`CS §`) — cite rules in the PR + review.
- SE-Architect Review Agent `8sYi523i4t` — adversarial lenses + Done gates.

## 7 🔗 References
- Addendum §B/§C/§7 `5t8uxVnnA4` (FR-PA3, FR-PA6, FR-BC1/BC2)
- Viability verdict `V6hlDjecfh`; build-vs-buy `IgOjzk034v`

## 8 🔗 Dependencies
- Blocked by ENG-2689 (spike) + ENG-2734 (counsel GO).
- Blocks ENG-2718 (ChatGPT) + ENG-2719 (Gemini) adapter build; feeds ENG-2690 contract.

## 9 📡 Protocol
On PR/artifact: cite `CS §` + FR-PA6; attach the per-provider live-DOM recordings + the GO/DEGRADE decision doc. Comment on this issue, cross-ref ENG-2689 + ENG-2719.
